query: Argentina EMBI+ spread daily values May 2026 Ambito tracker recent
answer:
## Argentina EMBI+ Spread (Riesgo País) — Daily Values, May 2026

### Overview

The Argentina EMBI+ spread, as calculated by JP Morgan and tracked by Ambito Financiero and other Argentine financial media, has shown significant compression in early May 2026. The dominant catalyst was **Fitch Ratings upgrading Argentina's sovereign credit rating from CCC+ to B-** on May 6, 2026, which triggered a sharp rally in Argentine bonds and a ~7% single-day drop in the spread.

---

### Late April 2026 Context

| Date | Close (bps) | Notes |
|------|-------------|-------|
| Apr 7 | **610** | Elevated by financial tensions |
| Apr 8 | **570** | Sharp drop; bond rally |
| Apr 11 | ~**550** | Middle East ceasefire optimism |
| Apr 13 | **528** | Strong bond rally |
| Apr 15 | **527** | Continued compression |
| Apr 17 | **518** | Lowest in April; Caputo's Washington visit |
| Apr 20 | **526** | Profit-taking rebound |
| Apr 22 | **535** | Volatility session |
| Apr 23 | **549** | Cautious markets |
| Apr 24 | **557** | Continued widening |
| Apr 27 | **582** | Selling pressure |
| Apr 28 | **587** | Peak late-April level |
| Apr 30 | **567** | Month-end improvement |
| **April close** | **556** | Bloomberg Línea end-of-month figure |

---

### May 2026 Daily Values

| Date | Close (bps) | Notes |
|------|-------------|-------|
| May 1 (Thu) | **539** | Broke below 540; Labor Day holiday in AR but JP Morgan still calculates |
| May 2 (Fri) | ~**539** | Weekly close held steady |
| May 5 (Mon) | **558** | Profit-taking rebound in bonds |
| **May 6 (Tue)** | **514** | **Fitch upgrade day (CCC+ → B-)**; ~7% single-day compression; ADRs surged up to 11% |
| May 7 (Wed) | **522–523** | Profit-taking; slight rebound from 514 |
| **May 8 (Thu)** | **510–513** | **Latest value; 3-month low**; intraday low ~510, close ~513 per Ambito |

---

### Current/Latest Value (May 8, 2026)

**~510–513 basis points** — a 3-month low, per JP Morgan's calculation as reported by Ambito Financiero.

---

### Key Trend Observations

1. **March 2026 peak**: The spread rose above 600 bps (hitting ~631 on March 30) driven by global volatility and Middle East geopolitical tensions.
2. **April trajectory**: Declined from 610 → 518 (mid-April), reversed to 587 (late April), then recovered to close at 556.
3. **May trajectory**: Opened at 539, spiked to 558 on profit-taking, then **collapsed to 514 on the Fitch upgrade** (May 6). After a brief rebound to 522–523 (May 7), it resumed its decline to 510–513 (May 8).
4. **Fitch upgrade catalyst**: The upgrade from CCC+ to B- was the dominant driver of the May compression, signaling reduced short-term default risk.

### Regional Comparison (End of April 2026, Bloomberg Línea)

| Country | EMBI Spread (bps) |
|---------|-------------------|
| Uruguay | 62 |
| Chile | 83 |
| Paraguay | 108 |
| Peru | 120 |
| Brazil | 174 |
| Colombia | 232 |
| Bolivia | 408 |
| Ecuador | 411 |
| **Argentina** | **556** |

---

### Notes on Ambito Tracker

The Ambito Financiero riesgo país tracker at `ambito.com/contenidos/riesgo-pais.html` loads data dynamically via JavaScript from `mercados.ambito.com`. The historical data tool requires interactive date selection. Daily values were cross-referenced across Ambito articles, Perfil, Infobae, Bloomberg Línea, and other Argentine financial media.

---

key_facts: Argentina EMBI+ spread stood at ~510-513 bps on May 8, 2026, a 3-month low per JP Morgan / Ambito Financiero.
Fitch Ratings upgraded Argentina's sovereign credit rating from CCC+ to B- on May 6, 2026, triggering a ~7% one-day spread compression.
May 6 saw the spread drop from 558 to 514 bps; Argentine ADRs surged up to 11% on the same day.
The spread briefly rebounded to 522-523 bps on May 7 (profit-taking) before declining again to 510-513 on May 8.
April 2026 closed at 556 bps (Bloomberg Línea); the month saw a range of 518-610 bps.
March 2026 peak was ~631 bps (March 30), driven by Middle East geopolitical tensions.
Argentina's EMBI+ spread remains the highest in Latin America, far above Brazil (174 bps), Colombia (232 bps), and Ecuador (411 bps) as of end-April 2026.
The Ambito Financiero riesgo país tracker loads data dynamically from mercados.ambito.com; historical data requires interactive date selection.
